content.problemStatement:
Frieza wants to become immortal. In order to do that he must acquire all the seven dragon balls. In a legendary fight, he is able to defeat Kami, the Guardian of the dragon balls. Now he has come across a case that contains the dragon balls and he needs your help to open it.

The case requires a pin number to be unlocked. And the clever Kami has left a puzzle on the case which must be cracked to unlock the case.

On the case, is displayed a string, SSS of length NNN consisting of lowercase alphabets. To unlock, he needs to convert this string into a pin number RRR.

The trick to creating that pin lies in his name [f,r,i,e,z,a][f, r, i, e, z, a][f,r,i,e,z,a]. Every character in SSS is either goodgoodgood or badbadbad. It is goodgoodgood, if it is a part of the letter set [f,r,i,e,z,a][f, r, i, e, z, a][f,r,i,e,z,a]. It is badbadbad otherwise. He'll have to traverse SSS one by one and count consecutive letters that are goodgoodgood or badbadbad and then store these corresponding counts in the same order in RRR. Thus the pin number, RRR will be a concatenation of the counts of these letters. See Sample Input for more details.

You have to help him find out RRR.

###Input:


The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case contains a single string SSS consisting of lowercase characters [a−z][a-z][a−z].
###Output:
For each test case, output in a single line answer denoting the decoded string which Frieza can use to attain immortality.

###Constraints


1≤T≤51 \leq T \leq 51≤T≤5
1≤N≤1051 \leq N \leq 10^51≤N≤105
SSS consists of only lower case alphabets.
constraints: S consists of only lowercase alphabets
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single string S consisting of lowercase characters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the decoded string which Frieza can use to attain immortality.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: The string S is "krillinisdead". The letter, "k" does not belong to the set [f,r,i,e,z,a] and hence its count, i.e., 1 is appended to the string R. "ri" belongs to the set [f,r,i,e,z,a] and hence 2 is appended to R. "ll" doesn't belong to the set [f,r,i,e,z,a], so its count 2 is appended to the string R.

solution.approach: The approach is to iterate over the input string and count consecutive occurrences of characters that are either good (part of the set [f,r,i,e,z,a]) or bad (not part of the set). The counts are then appended to the result string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum length of the input string. This is because we are iterating over each character in the input string for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) where N is the maximum length of the input string. This is because we are storing the result string which can be at most as long as the input string.

code.Python3:
for _ in range(int(input())):
    S = input()
    i, count = 1, 1
    prev = S[0]
    good = prev in 'frieza'
    while i < len(S):
        if (S[i] in 'frieza') == good:
            count += 1
        else:
            print(count, end='')
            count = 1
            good = S[i] in 'frieza'
        i += 1
    print(count)
